steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: On the left: “In many languages, including English, these consonants are rare” is an independent clause. On the right: “in some dialects of Mandarin, however, four such consonants are relatively common” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the independent clause punctuation rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses that are closely related or contrasting.

Choice C uses a semicolon here, which correctly handles this rule.

The other choices violate it: choice A creates a comma splice, choice B creates a run-on sentence, and choice D creates an ungrammatical structure.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
